Hey, this is Russell again and I wanted to record a video to show you what we do with our Info Product Funnels.
This Info Product Funnel I'm going to show you is kind of a free plus shipping offer, but you can do very similar things even if you're not selling a free plus shipping offer.
If you've heard me speak before, you know that I'm a huge, huge, huge proponent for a lot of different reasons and I'll kind of walk you through that.
Let me show you first the layout of this page.
If you notice the page has got headlines, it's got a countdown clock, headline video for me.
This video basically, I'm talking about this free book that I got, a negative free copy of it.
Here's the info on the offer real quick here.
Then we also have bullet points about everything you're going to learn in the book and testimonials.
Basically I just did bullet points testimonials just to get people excited about what's in it.
I'm not a big bullet point fan typically, but on a free plus shipping offer it is really cool just to have tons and tons of what they're going to learn inside.
Then I like talking about here's what you're going to do next, what's the catch.
People always wonder where the catch is.
I then come in and add in some urgency and then my guarantee and then have them ship the book.
Then testimonials down below.
That's kind of how we structured this offer right here.
I want to show you guys that real quick.
Now again, a free plus shipping offer, typically we lose some money because we just say pay shipping handling and then we ship it out of the book.
What we found is we usually get three to five times higher conversion rate doing this.
Let's say if we sold this as a $37 product and we drove a bunch of traffic.
If I got 100 people to pay it $37, I'm typically going to get like 300 to buy the free plus shipping.
Three times as many customers through the front door.
What we do is we have this thing called an order form bump.
Let me show you this.
The model is asking where to ship it to.
They put their shipping info.
Then they put in all their stuff here.
Then ship step number two.
Then we ask them for their credit card.
This is what we found is if we put this, this is called an order form bump.
If we put this above the credit card field, it didn't work.
They had to go put in all their credit card and then it's like really quick between the credit card form and then the button, we give them a real quick offer.
It says yes, I'll take it.
One time offer.
Want our conversion crush or video sales with a template.
It's proven to work and easy install.
Click yes to add this to your order now for just $37.
This offer is not available at any other time or place.
Those are like what two or three sentences right here.
Very simple, very easy to add a $37 bump onto their order.
Now we get about 38% of the people who buy this to take this bump.
What that means is if I was selling info product for $37, again, doing the free plus shipping, I get three times as many customers that come through the front door, but then a third of them, more than a third right now are taking this.
I make the exact same amount of upfront cash, but then I have three times as many people seeing my upsell and downsell flow.
It's a pretty powerful technique that we do.
We've done this on tons of different offers.
We add this little order form bump.
On the low end, we've seen 20% conversion on the high end, 40% to 50% conversion.
It's definitely worth trying to figure out how you can add this to every one of your offers.
Again, this is the front page of our free plus shipping offer.
After they put their credit card, then we take them to our first upsell.
Now, what we found is that our upsell, we have to have high enough price point that we're going to be able to cover our ad costs and make a profit.
Anything in the market we're in depends on the price point.
I like to have $197 for our first upsell and $297 for our second upsell.
Those are the two prices that I try to sell things at in our upsell, downsell, flow.
The second thing that's very important, especially with info products, is you have to really understand the psychology of what somebody just purchased.
When they come to a page, they came right here to buy this product.
They're learning about conversion, and we're going to give you all these conversion tests.
The second that they buy that, right now the conversion problem is not solved for them.
In their mind, it is.
They said, I have a conversion problem.
I just bought this.
Okay, cool.
That problem is now solved.
The worst possible thing you can sell on this second page for the upsell is more conversion stuff.
If you're selling a copywriting course, don't sell more copywriting stuff.
If you're selling a weight loss course, don't sell more weight loss stuff.
Whatever they just bought from you initially, just scratch that itch.
The next thing, if you try to offer more of the same thing, they're going to be like, well, I just bought this thing that was supposed to do that.
It really hurts the conversions.
I've seen other people who will say something like, hey, you just bought this, but if you want it to really work, you've got to have this as well.
That gets your customers really upset as well.
What I try to do is I try to look at what are all the things that my customers are going to experience when they purchase from me.
They're buying a conversion thing.
Now you've got conversion working.
Well, what's the next logical thing they need?
Now that their site converts, they're probably going to be more traffic.
For us, we shift over to our first upsell, which is all about traffic.
First thing is I think for ordering the product, and then I talk about, hey, we've got this other product that's going to help.
Now that you've got conversion figured out now, now I'm going to help you get more traffic to your site.
That way, you'll make more money because you'll be converting better from the first product you just bought, and now you're getting more traffic.
See how I tie those two things together?
It's very important to tie those two things together.
For a long time, we would just have random upsells that really didn't have anything to do with the front end product.
They were just like, hey, you bought this, here's some other random product.
Those don't convert well either.
If you want to get 20%, 30% conversions, it has to make logical sense to them.
It has to tie together.
This page is very simple.
We just doubled your conversions.
We want to double your traffic.
Video is selling them on that.
We have a 15 minute countdown clock. 15 minutes it ends, but we don't do anything.
That just gives us a little bit of urgency, and then we have them upgrade their offer.

If they buy it, they get the signal physically shipped out to them.

Especially in the price point, it's really high, like 300 bucks.
A lot of people may want to get that, but maybe they just spent $37 on your bump and then they spent $200 starting in $250 and they're like, I can't spend it at 300.
My wife's going to kill me.
That's the mentality a lot of times.
On this one, if they say no, then I take them to a down sell.
The down sell says wait and you help me finance it for you.
We've done down sells a couple of different ways.
One way is we do it where we give them a digital, if it's a physical product, you give them a digital version.
Sometimes if it's already a digital product, then we'll just finance it.
Instead of doing one payment of $297, we do three pay of $97.
On this down sell page, we did two things.
If you'll notice here, which actually ended up working really good.
We give the payment options, the financing options, so three payments of $97, which I say another 10 to 12% of people take that, which is kind of cool.
We also add the cart here.
In this video, what I do is I tell them, I basically say, hey, if you weren't able, if you said yes or you said no on the last page, the only logical reason is because you don't have the money to do it.
That's the only thing.
I want to make that easier for you.
Someone who gives you payment plans, you can get started today.
Then at the end of it, I say, by the way, here's a copy of the product and I show it and I say, look, what I'm going to do is that if you weren't sure about this offer for whatever reason, what I'm going to do is that if you pay it full, I'm going to give you special access to this one thing that only you're going to get because you're paying full right now.
What happens is we get another big percentage of people who said no on the last page, then say yes here because of the curiosity factor.
They want that one thing that they'll miss if they get the payment option.
That's the down sell page that we had a great, have had really great success with as well.

A lot of people say, where does the coaching funnel fit into this whole thing?
It fits right here on the thank you page for one.
Then the second place we do it is through the email follow-up sequence.
We also start pushing them into the coaching application page.
That's kind of our process.
We take someone to an info product funnel.
We usually have a front end that's free plus shipping or very low price.
We have this page right here.
They bump them up to $197 offer.
Then to a $297 offer, we downgrade to a payment option if they can afford that.
The thank you page where we give them access where they got and we try to push them over into our coaching funnel.
That's kind of how we do all of our info product funnels.
